The Utility expects customer electric load to increase in coming years primarily as a result of data center usage, electric vehicle adoption, and building electrification.

capex_mentions.quote: Total capital expenditures recorded in 2025 were $13.4 billion
forecasted to be $12.4 billion for 2026, $13.4 billion for 2027, $15.4 billion for 2028, $16.3 billion for 2029, and $16.0 billion for 2030
capex_mentions.context: Multi-year capex forecast including data center transmission and automation, not AI-specific

limitations: AI investment intensity: capex figures are total utility capex, not disaggregated for AI or data center infrastructure specifically; data center transmission investment is mentioned qualitatively but not quantified separately, limiting score to 3.

AI narrative concreteness: PG&E's AI references are primarily as a demand driver (data centers) and a risk vector, not as an internal operational AI initiative; no named AI programs or deployment details disclosed.

AI risk disclosure: risk-section material is present and names AI/generative AI as a cybersecurity threat vector with some mechanism detail (facilitating cyberattacks, data compromise), but no quantification or named governance body, capping at 3.
